Biography
Biography
Miguel Fuentes-Cabrera is an associate teaching professor in the Khoury College of Computer Sciences at Northeastern University, based in Oakland.
Before joining Khoury College in 2023, Fuentes-Cabrera built his research career at the Oak Ridge National Laboratory in Tennessee, where he used computational physics techniques to investigate nanomaterials. His research combines simulation and data science, with deep learning techniques parsing the data to improve simulation models and pave the way toward autonomous experiments. Most recently, he has studied diblock copolymers, microbial populations, and proteinaceous structures, among other topics. Along the way, he earned a Fulbright Scholarship, co-patented a self-assembly patterning method for organic molecules, and had his research cited more than 2000 times.
At Khoury College, Fuentes-Cabrera excitedly mentors students and teaches foundational computing topics, including discrete structures and data programming. When he’s not in the classroom, he can be found rock climbing, whitewater kayaking, or mountain biking.
